Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Noq: n0's new QUIC implementation in Rust 157 upvotes, 20 comments
  2. Return of the Obra Dinn: spherical mapped dithering for a 1bpp first-person game 249 upvotes, 36 comments
  3. The day I discovered type design 47 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Full Disclosure: A Third (and Fourth) Azure Sign-In Log Bypass Found 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. NanoGPT Slowrun: 10x Data Efficiency with Infinite Compute 104 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. From Oscilloscope to Wireshark: A UDP Story (2022) 80 upvotes, 16 comments
  7. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 180 upvotes, 56 comments
  8. My Random Forest Was Mostly Learning Time-to-Expiry Noise 22 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. Show HN: Three new Kitten TTS models – smallest less than 25MB 337 upvotes, 122 comments
  10. Launch HN: Voltair (YC W26) – Drone and charging network for power utilities 60 upvotes, 21 comments
  11. Be intentional about how AI changes your codebase 72 upvotes, 28 comments
  12. Launch HN: Canary (YC W26) – AI QA that understands your code 40 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. Scaling Karpathy's Autoresearch: What Happens When the Agent Gets a GPU Cluster 129 upvotes, 60 comments
  14. “Your frustration is the product” 454 upvotes, 262 comments
  15. Astral to Join OpenAI 1239 upvotes, 770 comments
  16. EsoLang-Bench: Evaluating Genuine Reasoning in LLMs via Esoteric Languages 68 upvotes, 32 comments
  17. How the Turner twins are mythbusting modern technical apparel 127 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Juggalo makeup blocks facial recognition technology (2019) 239 upvotes, 143 comments
  19. Cockpit is a web-based graphical interface for servers 186 upvotes, 110 comments
  20. Push events into a running session with channels 178 upvotes, 105 comments
  21. Clockwise acquired by Salesforce and shutting down next week 84 upvotes, 48 comments
  22. An update on Steam / GOG changes for OpenTTD 282 upvotes, 193 comments
  23. Drugwars for the TI-82/83/83 Calculators 45 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. Wayland set the Linux Desktop back by 10 years? 171 upvotes, 146 comments
  25. Waymo Safety Impact 240 upvotes, 231 comments
  26. Bombarding gamblers with offers greatly increases betting and gambling harm 86 upvotes, 74 comments
  27. Google details new 24-hour process to sideload unverified Android apps 558 upvotes, 643 comments
  28. Cover Flow with Modern CSS: Scroll-Driven Animations in Action (2025) 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. How many branches can your CPU predict? 31 upvotes, 30 comments
  30. 4Chan mocks £520k fine for UK online safety breaches 282 upvotes, 456 comments

Last generated 11:00am, 20 March 2026. Source here.
